title: Sale and Supply of Alcohol Act 2012

sections.title: Sale of alcohol to minors prohibited
sections.content: A licensee or manager must not allow alcohol to be sold or supplied to any person under the purchase age (18 years). Maximum penalty: $10,000.

sections.title: Supply of alcohol to minors prohibited
sections.content: No person may supply alcohol to a person under the purchase age unless they are the parent or guardian and the supply is responsible.

sections.title: Sale or supply of alcohol to intoxicated people
sections.content: A licensee or manager must not allow alcohol to be sold or supplied to a person who is intoxicated. Maximum penalty: $10,000.

sections.title: Police powers of entry
sections.content: A constable may enter any licensed premises at any reasonable time for the purpose of inspecting the premises and ensuring compliance with this Act.

sections.title: Power to require production of licence
sections.content: A constable may require the licensee or manager of licensed premises to produce the licence for inspection.

sections.title: Power to remove persons
sections.content: A constable or a person acting with the authority of the licensee or manager may remove from licensed premises any person who is intoxicated, violent, quarrelsome, or disorderly.

sections.title: Power of search and seizure
sections.content: A constable who has reasonable grounds to believe that an offence against this Act has been committed may search any person, vehicle, or thing and may seize any item that may be evidence.

sections.title: Controlled purchase operations
sections.content: Police may conduct controlled purchase operations to test compliance with the Act. This involves using persons under the purchase age to attempt to purchase alcohol.
